## **Review Themes** {#review-themes}

### Peloton - Fitness & Workouts {#review-app-1}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 24, 2026 - Jun 24, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Ryan Wong_, Jun 24, 2026, 2/5: "workouts and classes are good, but functionality on Google TV is extremely poor. App continuously crashes 90% of the time, making it very difficult to even take a class. Please fix!!!"

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      1 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: May 13, 2026 - May 13, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Ellis Heindselman_, May 13, 2026, 1/5: "A required $50 a month membership is a lot, it's more than my gym membership which includes a spin class a lot.. I don't understand why you wouldn't have tiered services for people that just want to use one machine or something like that... that doesn't include live classes but maybe includes old recorded ones... this is just too expensive."

   **Feature requests.**
      1 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jun 12, 2026 - Jun 12, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Crystal Carter_, Jun 12, 2026, 2/5: "The recent May update does not connect the Heart Rate and the leaderboard screen stays displayed which takes up a lot of screen space. Also, when you start the app, it lets you know that Peloton needs an update to the latest Android version. Please fix because HR is the best feature while working out."

   **Notifications and reminders.**
      1 recent review(s) mention notifications and reminders.
      Review window: Jun 11, 2026 - Jun 11, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention reminders or notifications. In productivity niches, this often points to retention mechanics.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Nikki Weaver_, Jun 11, 2026, 2/5: "The app in my pixel watch 3 won't connect to my Peloton bike. Recently the app won't connect to my bike for the HR and says "this app needs permission to track activity, issue notifications, and read sensor data." When i click ok, it says"enable sensor and other permissions on your watch to track your heart rate". Then it won't let me update the permission for "fitness and wellness". Everything else is allowed. It was fine right before Google updated the Fitbit app to Google Health."

   **Sync and accounts.**
      1 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: May 29, 2026 - May 29, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Keith L_, May 29, 2026, 2/5: "So frustrating. I moved from the UK to Canada. Have now spent hours on the phone with Peloton trying to move my account to Canada. 5 months later, they continue to charge my Canadian credit card in GBP. Every single customer support person swears they will solve the problem and then they don't. Why don't they just tell me to give up, cancel my account, and start over."
---

### Fitbod: Rutinas Personalizadas {#review-app-2}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: May 11, 2026 - Jul 3, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Hassan_, May 11, 2026, 1/5: "I encounter a significant bug in the Fitbod calendar. Even if my 4/4 objective is achieved, the bad sessions appear as completed. Targeted exercises and the “max effort” phase also seem bugged. Support has not provided any real solution for several days. I was only asked to uninstall/reinstall the app, which is frustrating for a paid app. I'm paying for a reliable app, not for having to fix bugs myself."
            2. _Lord Mute_, Jul 3, 2026, 2/5: "App is glitchy whenever you have a music engine like Spotify play in the background. Consistently has shown to have malfunctioning timers that quit after locking your phone. Record keeping is frustrating to correct and update suggested weights for workouts. Overall, this app is not worth the subscription rate it charges. It is mediocre at best and discouraging at worst."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      6 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jun 20, 2026 - Jul 16, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Alexander Christea_, Jul 7, 2026, 3/5: "there should be a 1.99 usd/month option too with limited ads, it is way too expensive"
            2. _Sarah Roberson_, Jul 6, 2026, 3/5: "Looks like it woulda been great. Took the time to fill everything out then couldn't proceed without accepting a free 7 day trial. Meaning I'll have to pay later. Wish that transparency was up front so I didn't waste my time filling everything out. I don't like having to give my information, then surprise... can't have it without paying. Feels untrustworthy to give money. Knowing up front - I know what I'm getting into."
            3. _Anthony Hough_, Jul 16, 2026, 4/5: "great app for tracking progress and developing training plans. however its quite expensive in comparison to similar apps"

   **Sync and accounts.**
      3 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jun 22, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _No Body_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "no longer a free option, and I can't delete the account unless I choose a paid option. I want nothing to do with this"
            2. _Steve Parsons_, Jun 22, 2026, 2/5: "THIS IS A PAY APP, AND A CRAFTY, DISHONEST ONE. At no point during the signing up are you informed this is a pay app... now you're at "Choose payment method." You cannot delete your account until you're logged in, and you cannot log in without submitting payment information. So, now these Jokers have your name, your email address, the equipment you own that you had to list (for a "personalized w/o routine") and all of THAT is being sold to Fitbod's 'partners'. TRUST: It is a mistake signing up"
            3. _Selena Shopping_, Jul 5, 2026, 4/5: "almost perfect... I just wished it was more optimized for women, yes you have pregnancy exercises but you don't account for postpartum women with diastasis recti that can't do regular exercises and please add more resistance bands exercises that uses loop band or handle bands that don't need any anchor ( like an optimized version that uses a chair or the body as an anchor... that's my only critique"

   **Feature requests.**
      1 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jul 5, 2026 - Jul 5, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Selena Shopping_, Jul 5, 2026, 4/5: "almost perfect... I just wished it was more optimized for women, yes you have pregnancy exercises but you don't account for postpartum women with diastasis recti that can't do regular exercises and please add more resistance bands exercises that uses loop band or handle bands that don't need any anchor ( like an optimized version that uses a chair or the body as an anchor... that's my only critique"

### Strava: Løpe, Sykle, Fottur {#review-app-3}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      2 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026

That doesn't work. After double, triple checking the code matches the email sent. Nope. "PLEASE TRY AGAIN WITH A NEW CODE." This has been an issue for several months. Very embarassing to see from a company as large as Strava. Two thumbs down 👎"
            2. _Tom B_, Jul 19, 2026, 2/5: "The app seems to have an issue that causes it to crash when the native camera app is used. With Strava running in the background you don't know it's crashed and stopped tracking. Phone is Pixel 10"

      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

I used to recommend this to my friends, but I'll be warning them all to avoid now."

Random people must be putting them in wrong."

I used to recommend this to my friends, but I'll be warning them all to avoid now."

### Lasta: Your Wellness Coach {#review-app-5}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      2 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 8, 2026 - Jun 13, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Mel_, Jun 13, 2026, 1/5: "Full application bug. Attempt to withdraw a second amount without my authorization of more than 60 euros. beware. but the worst is only 2 months later. he still tries to steal amounts between 50 and 60 euros from you. No expiry date, I blocked the card. to flee"
            2. _Acksone Laura Hanvichid_, Jun 8, 2026, 5/5: "I've tried several apps but this one is the right one for me. I was afraid it would be too difficult but the movements are approachable and dynamic. The sessions are short as promised but it's so good that I'm motivated to do more when I have time. I appreciate that there is no need to use accessories or a wall. I'm in the first week. The application is fluid, no bugs yet. Haven't lost weight yet but it still feels good."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      3 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jun 11, 2026 - Jul 17, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Dale White_, Jul 17, 2026, 1/5: "DO NOT believe anything they advertise My experience was similar to the other one star reviews. It's too bad I did not read them before purchasing. I thought I was getting a 28-day plan. It was only after checking PayPal did I discover that I was signed up for a monthly subscription. There is nothing in the payment screen that even hint at a subscription. When questioned, they agree to cancel future payments, but refused to refund the initial payment saying it is non-refundable."
            2. _Elle Pee_, Jul 17, 2026, 1/5: "BUYER BEWARE!!! Dont sign up with Lasta app because their monthly charges are exorbitant. Even when you cancel your subscription they still charge you and wont refund your money. Instead they negotiate and refund 50% in exchange for extending to reel you in so they can charge you again in succeeding months. STAY AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY!!!"
            3. _Sarah Butterfield_, Jul 16, 2026, 1/5: "too expensive"

   **Sync and accounts.**
      1 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jul 17, 2026 - Jul 17, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Elizabeth DeWiggins_, Jul 17, 2026, 5/5: "When I needed assistance with my account, Emma was very quick to respond and resolve the problem."

### Google Health (Fitbit) {#review-app-6}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      7 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 15, 2026 - Jul 20, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Hennesey Family_, Jul 20, 2026, 1/5: "update 7/26: it gets worse with every UI update. Functionality (i.e. sleep score) is broken half the time, and the UI isn't as good as Fitbit. this app is garbage compared to the Fitbit app. when my sensor breaks switching brands."
            2. _Sergey Solod_, Jul 20, 2026, 1/5: "It's like the app was developed by an inexperienced junior - it constantly freezes. And it's lagging! Shame. PS Their answer sounds - how can I put this - funny. They recommended that I restart the phone and relaunch the app. Google Team, please, train your answering bot to be less generic."
            3. _Dana_, Jul 20, 2026, 1/5: "Very buggy and overall too convoluted compared to the original fitbit app. Not sure how much money google offers these companies to sell out, but it's gotta be a lot when things change exponentially for the worse and then stay that way no matter how much pushback they receive. I've tried all the clearly AI prompted answers from the comments here in the responses to poor reviews and nothing has helped. I hate leaving bad reviews, but this app deserves it, unfortunately"

   **Notifications and reminders.**
      2 recent review(s) mention notifications and reminders.
      Review window: Jul 20, 2026 - Jul 20, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention reminders or notifications. In productivity niches, this often points to retention mechanics.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Jade Keswick_, Jul 20, 2026, 1/5: "I mainly have a fitbit to keep an eye on my heart rate and do ECG's as I have heart problems. The new app won't let me access the ECG reports. I've spent hours trying to figure it out and play with access requirements and zero luck. I also rely heavily on my watch for notifications but it regularly just stops sending them. Added: this is not an issue with the watch. It is running the ecgs fine. The app is not exporting the reports!"
            2. _Kevin Beckman_, Jul 20, 2026, 2/5: "Sends repeated notifications of workouts, sometimes seconds apart. Annoying."

   **Sync and accounts.**
      1 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jul 15, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Thomas FILOGRANA_, Jul 15, 2026, 1/5: "I have a Google Pixel, I bought a Charge 6, and you know what ? I can't Sync them. Google's a joke'"

      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _A B_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "The new updates to the app have absolutely broken it. The smart weight option keeps resetting the numbers you enter to reflect the current workout. When I turn off the smart weight thing, the app does not remember any of the previous workout reps or weights. This app was simple and easy now like most things, they've made it far worse by trying to update things that didn't really need updating. I am actively looking for alternative apps at this point."
            2. _Garrett Clark_, Jun 11, 2026, 1/5: "The new update stinks. I don't want the AI messing with my workouts. You can turn it off but now the app is slow and clunky. Want the old version back please."

## **Opportunities** {#opportunities}

1. Reliability as differentiation: Google Health (Fitbit), Planet Fitness, Nike Training Club, MyFitnessPal: Calorie Counter — 32 reviews. Focus: win on stable core flows, crash-free updates, and careful regression testing.
2. Clearer pricing and value packaging: Fitbod: Rutinas Personalizadas, Lasta: Your Wellness Coach, Fitify: Fitness, Home Workout, Strava: Løpe, Sykle, Fottur — 19 reviews. Focus: make upgrade value explicit and avoid surprising existing paid users.
3. Trustworthy cross-device workflow: MyFitnessPal: Calorie Counter, Nike Training Club, Fitbod: Rutinas Personalizadas, Planet Fitness — 17 reviews. Focus: make login, backup, and sync predictable across devices.
4. Roadmap ideas from unmet needs: Gymverse: Gym Workout Planner, Sweat: Fitness App For Women, Peloton - Fitness & Workouts, Hevy - Gym Log Workout Tracker — 8 reviews. Focus: mine concrete requested workflows before adding broad feature surface.
5. Better notification control: Google Health (Fitbit), Peloton - Fitness & Workouts, Samsung Health — 4 reviews. Focus: give users timing, frequency, and intensity controls.
6. Cleaner monetization: Strava: Løpe, Sykle, Fottur — 1 reviews. Focus: reduce interruptions, separate paid clean mode, and avoid ads after purchase.
